title: Browser-visible runtime labels and permissive preview notes broaden exposure review
impact_summary: Frontend bundles and public runtime values can reveal operational detail, hostnames, and allowlist intent that deserve review.
why_this_matters: Config review often spans both code and shipped client context, not just backend settings files.
evidence_fixture: frontend bundle snapshot plus placeholder runtime and preview-host values
suggested_patch_summary: Keep operational detail server-side and narrow browser-visible runtime configuration to what the client truly needs.
verification_summary: Verified as safe because only synthetic labels and hosts are present and no permissive CORS behavior is enabled.
replay_regression_note: Show the settings panel and the scorecard to explain how config and frontend exposure affect a TrustScore summary.
